# C ABI Design

## Generated Header

The GraalVM native-image compiler generates the following header from `@CEntryPoint` annotations:

```c
// libduckdb_ili_native.h (auto-generated by GraalVM native-image)

#ifndef __LIBDUCKDB_ILI_NATIVE_H
#define __LIBDUCKDB_ILI_NATIVE_H

#include <graal_isolate.h>

#if defined(__cplusplus)
extern "C" {
#endif

int run_main(int argc, char** argv);

int ili_native_version(graal_isolatethread_t*, char**);

int ili_native_echo(graal_isolatethread_t*, char*, char**);

void ili_free_string(graal_isolatethread_t*, char*);

#if defined(__cplusplus)
}
#endif
#endif
```

## Function Pointer Typedefs (for dlopen)

```c
// libduckdb_ili_native_dynamic.h (auto-generated)

typedef int (*ili_native_version_fn_t)(graal_isolatethread_t*, char**);
typedef int (*ili_native_echo_fn_t)(graal_isolatethread_t*, char*, char**);
typedef void (*ili_free_string_fn_t)(graal_isolatethread_t*, char*);
```

## Memory Ownership

| Resource | Owner | Free via |
|---|---|---|
| `char*` returned by `ili_native_version` | Caller | `ili_free_string()` |
| `char*` returned by `ili_native_echo` | Caller | `ili_free_string()` |
| `char*` passed as `requestJson` | Caller | Caller manages |
| `char**` output pointers | Caller | Stack-allocated, no free needed |

## Isolate Lifecycle

The caller creates a GraalVM isolate before calling any function:

```c
graal_isolate_t *isolate = NULL;
graal_isolatethread_t *thread = NULL;
graal_create_isolate(NULL, &isolate, &thread);

// ... call functions ...

graal_tear_down_isolate(thread);
```

For DuckDB extension use, the isolate should be created once per extension load (not per function call).

## Future Streaming API

For large datasets (Phase 5+), a callback-based streaming API will be added:

```c
typedef int (*ili_row_callback)(void* user_data, const char** values, int value_count);

int ili_validate_xtf_stream(
    graal_isolatethread_t* thread,
    const char* request_json,
    ili_row_callback callback,
    void* user_data
);
```
